Identity.History: Memnon of Rhodes was a Greek mercenary commander serving the Achaemenid Persian Empire during Alexander the Great’s invasion. An experienced general, he advised using scorched-earth tactics in Anatolia and later led Persian naval resistance in the Aegean. After Alexander’s victory at Granicus in 334 BC, Memnon became one of Persia’s most dangerous commanders, threatening Macedonian supply lines and Greek support. His sudden death from illness in 333 BC removed a capable opponent and weakened Persian strategy against Alexander.
